dependencies: Reliance on third-party national distribution companies for food and supply delivery
Darden Direct Distribution, Inc. manages inventory through third-party distribution partners
Products sourced from more than 1,400 suppliers originating in more than 30 countries
Limited number of suppliers and distributors for certain products and services
risks: Shortages, delays or interruptions in supply of food items due to severe weather, natural disasters, macroeconomic conditions (tariffs and trade disputes), labor issues, vendor solvency
Supply chain disruptions have increased costs and limited product availability
Introduction of or changes to tariffs or restrictive trade policies on imported food products (produce, seafood) could increase costs and impact supply
Dependence on third-party vendors and distributors for timely delivery
Potential for food safety issues at source or by suppliers/distributors

regulatory_risks: Federal, state, local and international laws affecting restaurant operations (licensing, health, safety, fire regulations)
Zoning, land use, environmental, traffic regulations for site development
Alcoholic beverage licensing and regulations (minimum age, hours of operation, advertising, inventory control)
Dram shop statutes (liability for serving intoxicated persons)
Federal, state and local minimum wage laws and employment practices regulations (overtime, tip credits, working conditions, paid leave, safety standards, hiring practices)
U.S. Department of Labor Fair Labor Standards Act overtime rules and salary thresholds
Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and related state accessibility statutes
Employer-provided health insurance regulations
Food preparation and sale regulations (product safety, nutritional content, menu labeling, calorie/fat/allergen disclosure)
Information security, privacy, cashless payments, consumer credit protection and fraud laws (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard - PCI DSS)
Anti-corruption laws (Foreign Corrupt Practices Act) in U.S. and international jurisdictions
International franchising and intellectual property licensing laws
Federal and state environmental regulations (use, storage, discharge, emission, disposal of hazardous materials)
Climate change regulations (greenhouse gas emissions reduction, water consumption)
Immigration laws and work authorization requirements
Union organizing rights and labor relations laws
Unclaimed property laws
Changes in tax laws and regulations (federal, state, local income taxes)
SEC climate-related disclosure rules (currently stayed pending legal challenges)
Data privacy and data protection laws (evolving and increasingly demanding across jurisdictions)
Anti-ESG sentiment and related scrutiny from government institutions

sanction_risks: Imposition of retaliatory tariffs on important U.S. imports and exports
Restrictive trade policies or trade disputes impacting imported food products (produce, seafood)
Tariffs on imported food products could increase costs and impact supply
